channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: NEARstudios Sets Expectations for Hawthorn Release Date News
item.content:encoded:
Heather Cerlan of NEARstudios says more work remains before Hawthorn is ready to reveal a release date and launch plans.

Hawthorn is a very special game. Former developers from Bethesda, BioWare, and Naughty Dog have teamed up to bring players a Sandbox RPG that supports co-op or solo experiences as they build their villages. The game also has personality-rich NPCs. It was one of the many games featured in the May 2026 Insider Gaming Showcase. Now, the studio’s co-founder is offering an update on the status of a potential release date for the anticipated nature-driven sandbox.

NEARstudios Founder Has an Update for Fans Waiting on Hawthorn

As part of the Insider Gaming Showcase, Cerlan was interviewed by our Executive Editor Mike Straw. She explained that fan response to the game and the launch of its Kickstarter campaign served as a eureka moment, proving the team was on to something players would actually be interested in.

She also explained that she’s happy this game could serve as a form of escapism for players who may want to escape the uncertainty and negativity of the current news cycle.

When asked about a potential launch date, Cerlan says that the early access date remains a moving target. Funding remains key, as it allows them to keep pushing the game’s limits further. When they are ready to announce a date, the community will know ASAP.

“For us, it’s a rolling target because we’re just looking to bring in little bits of funding wherever we can so we can keep pushing our early access date out because obviously the more time we have, the better we can make the offering to players as this kind of initial rollout,” she said. “We don’t have any dates to announce, but I’ll just say that we just keep nudging it out as we get a little bit more.

“A little more additional funding here and there, whether we get, like, I mean, we’ve been able to do this through obviously the Kickstarter, but we’ve had a few angel investors that have given us very small checks; we’re talking like $50K or something like that. Just little bits of cash here and there to keep things rolling. So it’s one of those things where we’ve got a few different things we’re looking at, different options that we’re assessing right now. Depending on what choice we make, that would determine when that date will come.”

Insider Gaming will continue to monitor the story and report any updates as more information becomes available. You can Wishlist it and check out the demo on Steam. To check out a comprehensive list on Steam of all the games featured at the Insider Gaming Showcase on May 28, click here.

Are you interested in Hawthorn? Sound off about this story and all others in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Eggstinct Mixes Rocket League Tricks With Mario 64 Platforming
item.content:encoded:
Eggstinct is one of the many games featured as part of the recent Insider Gaming Showcase. Now, the game’s creator is sharing more about the classic games that influenced the forthcoming title.

On the surface, a game where you play as dinosaurs throwing eggs might seem like any Yoshi title or a Super Smash Bros. experience. However, Jesse Jenkins, the Founder of Manaforce Studios, has drawn inspiration from classic titles from decades past and more modern multiplayer hits to create a floppy dinosaur experience that players will hopefully enjoy with plenty of friends.

Eggstinct Is a Creative Mix of Rocket League and Mario 64

As part of the recent Showcase, Jenkins spoke to Insider Gaming’s Mike Straw and explained that the initial idea for the game stemmed from a desire to play a 1990s 3D platformer in multiplayer. Specifically, Jenkins cited Super Mario 64 and Spyro the Dragon as inspirations.

“The initial idea was we wanted to take classic platforming and make it like multiplayer physics-based. So, imagine Mario 64, Spyro, if you were able to play that with your friends, you know what I mean? That’s where we’ve headed. And you know, with all of that being said, we also want to make sure that we have kind of like a robust environment for cooperative and competitive gameplay. So that was kind of the initial hook. Okay. Multiplayer physics-based floppy dinosaur game.”

Furthermore, they added elements from Tony Hawk Pro Skater and Rocket League, including scoring mechanics and trick-based bonuses.

“To expand on it, we have created this kind of score system which takes a lot of inspiration from games like Rocket League and Tony Hawk. So, basically, all of the ways that you score in the game is you perform different kinds of tricks, and you launch the egg into the nest, and you get like these trick multipliers based on how you score your egg, right? We’re still in kind of the beginning phases of it, but it’s going to be more complex… also just really trying to focus on making that feel really, really fun and unique every time that you score an egg.”

Eggstinct currently doesn’t have a solidified release date. Players can currently request access to a playtest via Steam. To check out a comprehensive list on Steam of all the games featured at the Insider Gaming Showcase on May 28, click here.

Are you interested in Eggstinct? Sound off about this story and all others in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: NYT Connections Hints Today (#1086)—Answers for Monday, June 1
item.content:encoded:
Well, it’s the start of a whole new week and a new month today! It’s a cause for celebration, as we’re one major step closer to summertime officially kicking in. Below, you’ll find today’s NYT Connections hints and solutions to give you the best chances possible of coasting into summer with a perfect streak (hopefully).

Scroll down to get the NYT Connections answers you need!

How to Play NYT Connections

In June 2023, NYT Connections emerged as yet another game in the ever-growing lineup of the New York Times titles. This title soared immediately, and it’s easy to see why. As a free-to-play, accessible New York Times game, NYT Connections is available worldwide and can be played on desktop devices or in a mobile app.

But how do you play NYT Connections?

The NYT team will provide you with a grid of 16 words once a day. The only task left up to you is to sort those words into four groups of four by finding their commonalities. Every NYT Connections puzzle has just one solution, and some words can have double meanings, so it can sometimes be tricky to figure out the answers.

It’s also important to stress that the words are color-coded: yellow, blue, green, and purple. That’s in order of difficulty, from least to most.

What’s critical here is that you get four guesses every day, and if you use those, your NYT Connections answers for today will be out of reach, as it’ll be game over.

Today’s NYT Connections Hints (June 1)

Here are the words for today’s NYT Connections:

  • WEDDING
  • MENAGERIE
  • ONION
  • NEWSPAPER
  • CEILING
  • SLIPPERS
  • TREE
  • CAT
  • DOOR
  • TATTOO
  • PIPE
  • WALL
  • KEY
  • WINDOW
  • STREETCAR
  • ROBE

And here are the hints I can offer for today’s NYT Connections:

  • Yellow – Features of a room
  • Green – Things you’d lounge around with or in
  • Blue – Words from Tennessee Williams’ titles
  • Purple – Types of trees

Here’s the final warning before we get to the NYT Connections answers for today, June 1!

Are you sure you want to scroll further?

Very well!

NYT Connections Answers Explained for Today, June 1 (#1086)

If you want to jump straight to the answers for today’s NYT Connections puzzle, I’ve got you covered:

  • Yellow – CEILING, DOOR, WALL, WINDOW
  • Green – NEWSPAPER, PIPE, ROBE, SLIPPERS
  • Blue – CAT, MENAGERIE, STREETCAR, TATTOO
  • Purple – KEY, ONION, TREE, WEDDING

When Does NYT Connections Reset?

That’s today’s puzzle, but if you want the next one, the game resets daily at midnight local time (that’s your local time). You’ll have 24 hours to complete the puzzle when midnight ticks over in your timezone.

How did you do? Did you get a perfect run? Let me know what your streak looks like on the Insider Gaming Discord server

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/nyt-connections-hints-solutions-june-1-1086/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: ARC Raiders PvE ‘Rebellion’ Map Condition Being Tested in China
item.content:encoded:
Embark Studios has just deployed the first round of tests for an all-new Chinese version of ARC Raiders, the sensational extraction adventure title that was released in October 2025. It’s slated to come to China with a separate, amended version of the game, which is pretty typical for shooters dropping in the region.

Eagle-eyed fans have seemingly uncovered a new map condition called ‘Rebellion Incident’, which appears to reference a unique mode in which players cannot inherently damage other players until they instigate a deliberate ‘rebellion’. At which point they’re painted as traitors and marked by the lobby.

This is exactly the kind of map condition that PvE players dream of.

Is Embark Studios Testing More PvE Content for ARC Raiders?

The tests for ARC Raiders in China have uncovered two new map conditions that are being discussed on Chinese social media platforms. There’s a PvE-focused condition called Rebellion Incident and another called Double King (or Queen), which seems to introduce both a Queen and a Matriarch on the same map, at the same time.

Other names that have surfaced in translation are ‘Safe Zone’ and ‘Dual Boss’.

In a post on Western social media, one user admitted that ARC Raiders is not popular in China but that there is a ‘group of loyal fans’ exploring the new Chinese version of the game. They then provided screenshots of the new map conditions.

They explained, based on translations of content on a Chinese forum:

Rebellion event is almost a low-difficulty mode, but there are a lot of resources in this mode. In this mode, players default that friendly troops cannot harm each other, but they can choose “rebellion” and then the map will announce that there are rebels somewhere.

Further investigation reveals a mode that will paint ‘traitors’ with a red icon on the compass or in-game map when they betray their fellow raiders. This is exactly how PvE-focused fans of ARC Raiders envisioned such a mode surfacing, and it may appear on the traditional platform at some point.

Do you want ‘Rebellion Incident’ to appear in the core ARC Raiders experience? Let us know what you’re thinking on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that ARC Raiders finally won a Game of the Year award

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP
